2605.19662.title: When Tabular Foundation Models Meet Strategic Tabular Data: A Prior Alignment Approach
2605.19662.paper_summary: Tabular foundation models based on pretrained prior-data fitted networks~(PFNs) have shown strong generalization on diverse tabular tasks, but they are typically designed for \emph{non-strategic} settings where data distributions are independent of deployed classifiers. In many real-world decision scenarios, however, individuals may strategically modify their features after deployment to obtain favorable outcomes, inducing a post-deployment distribution shift. This paper studies whether PFN-style tabular foundation models can generalize to such \emph{strategic} tabular data. We show that strategic manipulation creates a mismatch between the non-strategic prior learned during pretraining and the post-manipulation strategic prior, which leads to systematic prediction bias. To address this issue, we propose \textbf{Strategic Prior-data Fitted Network}~\textit{(SPN)}, an inference-time strategy-aware framework that adapts tabular foundation models to strategic environments without retraining. SPN constructs strategic in-context examples to approximate post-manipulation inputs and aligns PFN predictions with the induced strategic distribution. Experiments on real-world and synthetic tabular datasets show that SPN consistently improves robustness and predictive performance under strategic manipulation compared with both tabular foundation models and classical tabular methods.
2605.19662.paper_summary_zh: 基於預訓練的先前數據擬合網絡~(PFNs)的表格基礎模型在各種表格任務上展現了強大的泛化能力，但它們通常是為\emph{非策略性}環境設計的，其中數據分佈與部署的分類器相互獨立。然而，在許多現實世界的決策情境中，個體可能會在部署後策略性地修改其特徵以獲得有利的結果，從而引起部署後的分佈變化。本文研究PFN風格的表格基礎模型是否能夠對這種\emph{策略性}表格數據進行泛化。我們表明，策略性操控在預訓練期間學習的非策略性先驗和後操控的策略性先驗之間造成了不匹配，這導致了系統性的預測偏差。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了\textbf{策略性先前數據擬合網絡}~\textit{(SPN)}，這是一個在推理時具有策略意識的框架，能夠在不重新訓練的情況下將表格基礎模型適應於策略性環境。SPN構建策略性的上下文示例以近似後操控的輸入，並將PFN的預測與引起的策略性分佈對齊。在現實世界和合成的表格數據集上的實驗顯示，與表格基礎模型和傳統表格方法相比，SPN在策略性操控下始終提高了穩健性和預測性能。
2605.19662.author: Xinpeng Lv et.al.
2605.19662.authors: Xinpeng Lv, Yunxin Mao, Renzhe Xu, Chunyuan Zheng, Yikai Chen, Haoxuan Li, Jinxuan Yang, Kun Kuang, Yuanlong Chen, Mingyang Geng, Wanrong Huang, Shixuan Liu, Shaowu Yang, Wenjing Yang, Zhouchen Lin, Haotian Wang
